prompt: Executive sponsors ask how GRC oversight will be sustained after go-live. Which structure should the program establish?
choices: A cross-functional GRC steering committee with defined meeting cadence and escalation thresholds
A single developer queue for all policy updates without business review
An ad hoc email thread whenever regulators publish new guidance
A quarterly spreadsheet circulated only to external auditors
explanation: Governance committees provide ongoing oversight, prioritization, and escalation for policy, risk, and compliance decisions.

prompt: Legal requires formal approval before internal policies publish to employees. Which workflow design supports governed policy lifecycle management?
choices: Draft, review, and publish states with approvers recorded before policies become effective
Immediate publish on save with no approval history retained
Manual PDF uploads stored outside ServiceNow without version control
Automatic retirement of every policy after thirty days regardless of status
explanation: Policy approval workflows enforce review and publication gates so only authorized versions become effective.

prompt: Business units interpret risk appetite differently for public-facing services versus back-office systems. How should enterprise risk governance align those views?
choices: Publish enterprise risk appetite statements and map business-unit thresholds to governed scoring models
Allow each unit to define incompatible scales without central review
Hide appetite settings from risk owners to reduce debate
Disable residual scoring until every unit agrees informally
explanation: Risk governance aligns business units to enterprise appetite through documented thresholds and consistent scoring methodology.

prompt: A city department wants compliance metrics visible to finance, HR, and IT without conflicting definitions. Which governance approach reduces silos?
choices: Align business units to shared control and risk definitions in a governed metric catalog
Let each department maintain separate spreadsheets with different field names
Restrict compliance visibility to a single administrator account
Export raw tables weekly without documented definitions
explanation: Organizational alignment depends on shared definitions, ownership, and reporting standards across GRC modules.

prompt: Leadership asks who may accept a control failure when remediation exceeds the current budget cycle. Which operating model element answers that question?
choices: Documented decision rights specifying who can approve exceptions, acceptance, and escalation
Default closure of every issue when the due date passes
Unlimited delegation to any catalog consumer without audit trail
Automatic deletion of failed control tests at month end
explanation: GRC operating models define who can accept risk, approve exceptions, and escalate unresolved control gaps.
